title: Update kicad-to-circuit-json to ^0.0.51
description: Updates the kicad-to-circuit-json dependency from 0.0.32 to 0.0.51 in package.json and refreshes bun.lock to reflect the new version.
body:
### Motivation
- Bump the `kicad-to-circuit-json` dependency to the latest published range so the workspace uses `0.0.51` with any upstream fixes or improvements.

### Description
- Update the root dependency range in `package.json` from `^0.0.32` to `^0.0.51`.
- Refresh `bun.lock` so the resolved package entry and checksum reflect `kicad-to-circuit-json@0.0.51`.
- Changes are limited to `package.json` and `bun.lock` and were committed.

### Testing
- Running `bun test` before building reported missing `../dist` (failing due to not-yet-built artifacts). 
- Running `bun run build` completed successfully and produced `dist` artifacts. 
- Running `bun test` after the build passed (2 tests, 0 failures).

title: add shape to autorouting phase regions
description: Adds a shape property to the autorouting phase regions, allowing for rectangular region definitions in autorouting.

title: make name optional in port
description: Makes the name property optional in portProps, allowing for ports to be defined without a name.

title: make direction optional for port
description: Makes the direction property optional for port components, allowing for more flexible port definitions without requiring a direction to be specified.

title: Support rectangular reroute phases in autoroutingphase
description: Adds reroute and region handling to autoroutingphase, allowing a later phase to rip previously routed traces inside a rectangular region, autoroute only the clipped regional connections, and reconnect the result back into the full route set.
body:
Adds reroute and region handling to `<autoroutingphase>`, allowing a later phase to rip previously routed traces
  inside a rectangular region, autoroute only the clipped regional connections, and reconnect the result back into the
  full route set.

title: Fix: inflated footprints where not presisted to their original layer
description: Fixes the issue where inflated footprints were not persisted to their original layer in the PCB design.

title: Fix: minViaEdgeToPadEdgeClearance was being passed min_pad_edge_to_pad_edge_clearance
description: Fixes incorrect assignment of minViaEdgeToPadEdgeClearance in autorouting calculations, ensuring proper clearance values are used.

title: Use port direction for group schematic box pin placement
description: Adds direction-based schematic box placement for group ports when no explicit schPinArrangement is provided, while preserving schPinArrangement as the override.
body: Adds direction-based schematic box placement for group ports when no explicit schPinArrangement is provided, while preserving schPinArrangement as the override. Updates Port to use shared props from @tscircuit/props, bumps the props dependency, and adds coverage for direction-based placement plus override behavior.

title: Add the method `unrouteCircuitJson`
description: Adds a method to filter out unrouted elements from circuit JSON data.

### Motivation
- Bring the local autorouter dependency up to the latest published version (`0.0.500`) for bug fixes and improved routing behavior.

### Description
- Updated `devDependencies` in `package.json` to change `@tscircuit/capacity-autorouter` from `^0.0.485` to `^0.0.500`.

### Testing
- Ran `bun install` which installed `@tscircuit/capacity-autorouter@0.0.500` successfully.
- Ran typecheck with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which completed without errors.
- Ran `bun test tests/features/autorouter-version-v4.test.tsx tests/features/autorouter-version-v3.test.tsx` and both tests passed.
- Built the package with `bun run build` and the build succeeded.

title: Fix named schPinArrangement ports for group schematic boxes
description: Fixes handling of named child ports in schPinArrangement for group schematic boxes, centralizing pin extraction and improving pin count calculations.
body: Allow group schematic boxes to use named child ports in schPinArrangement before child port labels are available. Centralizes explicit arrangement pin extraction with getPinsFromPortArrangement, handles string pins when computing pin counts, and adds coverage for side placement/facing behavior.

title: fix circuit json subcircuit anchor not working
description: Fixes the issue where the subcircuit anchor alignment does not function correctly in circuit JSON, ensuring proper positioning of autosized subcircuits.

title: Circuit Json subcircuit repro
description: Adds tests for rendering subcircuits with specific anchor alignments in circuit JSON.

title: Add autorouting phase component support
description: Adds a self-closing autoroutingphase  primitive for configuring autorouter behavior per existing routingPhaseIndex phase. Routing phase plans now attach matching phase autorouter configs, local autorouting uses those configs per phase, and net-derived trace phases reuse the existing net phase lookup.
body: Adds a self-closing <autoroutingphase /> primitive for configuring autorouter behavior per existing routingPhaseIndex phase. Routing phase plans now attach matching phase autorouter configs, local autorouting uses those configs per phase, and net-derived trace phases reuse the existing net phase lookup.

title: add schematic section
description: Add support for schematic sections and include a missing package for enhanced schematic organization.

description: Add polygon-aware DRC checks for rotated pads and plated holes

title: Add bugreport58 autorouting regression
description: Motivation Capture and preserve an autorouting bug report snapshot for regression prevention by downloading its simple_route_json payload. Provide a quick way to visually inspect the failing SRJ in Cosmos via a debugger fixture. Enable automated regression detection by adding a snapshot test that runs the solver and records the final SVG output.  Description Added the downloaded bug report data to fixturesbug-reportsbugreport58-b69d72bugreport58-b69d72.json containing the simple_route_json. Added a Cosmos debugger fixture at fixturesbug-reportsbugreport58-b69d72bugreport58-b69d72.fixture.tsx that renders AutoroutingPipelineDebugger with the SRJ. Added a Bun snapshot regression test testsbugsbugreport58-b69d72.test.ts and the generated SVG snapshot testsbugs__snapshots__bugreport58-b69d72.snap.svg which runs the AutoroutingPipelineSolver and snapshots the visualization.  Testing Ran bun test testsbugsbugreport58-b69d72.test.ts and the test passed (1 pass, 0 fail). Ran bun run format:check (biome format .) and it reported no unformatted files. The new files were committed and are ready for review.
body:
### Motivation

- Capture and preserve an autorouting bug report snapshot for regression prevention by downloading its `simple_route_json` payload. 
- Provide a quick way to visually inspect the failing SRJ in Cosmos via a debugger fixture. 
- Enable automated regression detection by adding a snapshot test that runs the solver and records the final SVG output.

### Description

- Added the downloaded bug report data to `fixtures/bug-reports/bugreport58-b69d72/bugreport58-b69d72.json` containing the `simple_route_json`. 
- Added a Cosmos debugger fixture at `fixtures/bug-reports/bugreport58-b69d72/bugreport58-b69d72.fixture.tsx` that renders `AutoroutingPipelineDebugger` with the SRJ. 
- Added a Bun snapshot regression test `tests/bugs/bugreport58-b69d72.test.ts` and the generated SVG snapshot `tests/bugs/__snapshots__/bugreport58-b69d72.snap.svg` which runs the `AutoroutingPipelineSolver` and snapshots the visualization.

### Testing

- Ran `bun test tests/bugs/bugreport58-b69d72.test.ts` and the test passed (1 pass, 0 fail). 
- Ran `bun run format:check` (`biome format .`) and it reported no unformatted files.
- The new files were committed and are ready for review.

------

body: ## Before ## After

title: Support pic_programmer round-trip
description: Add parser and serializer support needed for the pic_programmer KiCad demo board to round-trip successfully.
body:
## Summary
Add parser and serializer support needed for the `pic_programmer` KiCad demo board to round-trip successfully.

## What Changed
- added support for newer `setup` and `via` finish tokens used by the board, including `covering`, `plugging`, `capping`, `filling`, and nested `front`/`back` values
- added footprint support for `units`, `unit`, `name`, `pins`, `point`, `size`, and `duplicate_pad_numbers_are_jumpers`
- extended pad, segment, and via net parsing to handle name-only `(net "...")` forms used in the fixture
- added a dedicated `pic_programmer` round-trip test in `tests/sexpr/KicadPcbDemos.test.ts`

## Why
The `pic_programmer` fixture uses S-expression tokens and net forms that were not yet modeled in the parser, which caused parse failures before the round-trip assertion could run.

## Impact
This improves KiCad demo board compatibility for `pic_programmer` without changing the existing round-trip test flow.

## Validation
- `bun test tests/sexpr/KicadPcbDemos.test.ts -t "pic_programmer.kicad_pcb round-trip"`

title: add support for parsing everything in the arduino kicad repo
description: Adds support for parsing zones, rectangles, text boxes, and generator versions in KiCad schematic and symbol libraries, enhancing the parsing capabilities of the tscircuit library.

title: Add typed KicadPcb graphic collections
description: Add first-class KicadPcb collections for top-level arc, gr_arc, gr_circle, and gr_curve primitives, classifying those parsed children directly in KicadPcb instead of leaving them in otherChildren, and adding a focused parser test covering all four collections.
body:
## Summary
- add first-class `KicadPcb` collections for top-level `arc`, `gr_arc`, `gr_circle`, and `gr_curve` primitives
- classify those parsed children directly in `KicadPcb` instead of leaving them in `otherChildren`
- add a focused parser test covering all four collections

## Validation
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun test tests/sexpr/classes/KicadPcb.test.ts`

title: Update the kicad version to the latest 10.0.* and tscircuit pkg
description: This pull request updates the kicad version to the latest 10.0. and updates the tscircuit package dependencies in the project.

title: Support Edge.Cuts gr_circle with typed KicadPcb graphics
description: What changed added Edge.Cuts gr_circle support to board outline collection and PCB centering added a focused gr_circle Edge.Cuts PCB fixture and snapshot coverage switched the outline helpers to use typed public kicadts collections directly bumped kicadts to 0.0.30  Why Top-level gr_circle board graphics were not being included in pcb_board.outline, and the temporary local fallbacks around graphic primitives were only needed until kicadts exposed typed top-level graphic collections.  Impact Boards that use gr_circle on Edge.Cuts now produce the expected outline, and this repo now depends on the public typed kicadts API instead of local private-field fallbacks.  Validation bun test testspcbgr-circle-edge-cuts-fragment-pcb.test.ts testspcbgr-curve-edge-cuts-fragment-pcb.test.ts bunx tsc --noEmit
body:
## What changed

- added `Edge.Cuts gr_circle` support to board outline collection and PCB centering
- added a focused `gr_circle` Edge.Cuts PCB fixture and snapshot coverage
- switched the outline helpers to use typed public `kicadts` collections directly
- bumped `kicadts` to `^0.0.30`

## Why

Top-level `gr_circle` board graphics were not being included in `pcb_board.outline`, and the temporary local fallbacks around graphic primitives were only needed until `kicadts` exposed typed top-level graphic collections.

## Impact

Boards that use `gr_circle` on `Edge.Cuts` now produce the expected outline, and this repo now depends on the public typed `kicadts` API instead of local private-field fallbacks.

## Validation

- `bun test tests/pcb/gr-circle-edge-cuts-fragment-pcb.test.ts tests/pcb/gr-curve-edge-cuts-fragment-pcb.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`

title: Also consider inferred vias in trace-to-pad clearance relaxation
description: Adds functionality to consider inferred vias in trace clearance relaxation, addressing DRC regressions caused by layer transitions with empty vias.
body:
### Motivation
- Trace clearance relaxation only measured traces against explicit `route.vias`, missing implied vias created by layer transitions where the route point has the same XY but different `z` values.
- That caused traces to not be relaxed away from those implicit via centers and produced DRC regressions when a route had layer transitions but an empty `vias` array.

### Description
- Added `getRouteViaCenters` to collect via centers from both explicit `route.vias` and inferred via locations at zero-length layer transitions. (`lib/solvers/GlobalDrcForceImproveSolver/traceToPadClearanceRelaxation.ts`)
- Updated blocker collection and trace-to-other-route clearance computations to use `getRouteViaCenters` instead of iterating only `otherRoute.vias`, so trace clearance considers inferred vias as blockers.
- Added a regression test `moves a trace until its edge clears an inferred different-net via edge` to verify a trace is nudged away from an inferred different-net via center. (`tests/solver-route-behavior.test.ts`)

### Testing
- Added the unit test in `tests/solver-route-behavior.test.ts` covering the inferred via case; test logic is present in the repo.
- Attempted to run tests with `bun test` and `bun test tests/solver-route-behavior.test.ts`, but automated execution failed due to missing external dependencies (`@tscircuit/math-utils`, etc.) in the execution environment, so tests could not be executed here.
- Ran `git diff --check` and committed the changes as `Handle inferred vias in trace clearance relaxation`.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69fb3d8593448327a3bc1ced6877888c)

title: Add Via to Pad clearance solver
description: Adds a new solver for adjusting via to pad clearance in routing, enhancing the routing algorithms ability to manage clearance between vias and pads effectively.

title: Scope order dialog CSS to avoid host page conflicts
description: Scoping CSS variables and styles to the order dialog component to prevent global CSS conflicts with the host page.
body:
### Motivation
- Importing the package stylesheet was leaking global CSS (variables, box-sizing, body font/background) into the host site and changing white backgrounds to gray due to global selectors and `:root` variables.

### Description
- Move design tokens from `:root` to `.od-page-backdrop` so CSS variables only apply inside the order dialog component tree.
- Restrict `box-sizing` and other global rules to `.od-page-backdrop` and its descendants instead of using the universal `*` or touching `html`, `body`, or `#root`.
- Remove global `body`/`html` styles (min-height, margin, font-family, color, background, smoothing) and apply those to `.od-page-backdrop` instead.
- Scope tooltip selectors (`[data-tip]`) under `.od-page-backdrop` so host elements using `data-tip` are unaffected.

### Testing
- Ran `bun run typecheck` (TypeScript typecheck) and it succeeded.
- Ran `bun run build:preview` (Vite preview build) and it succeeded producing the `dist` output.
- Ran `git diff --check` to validate whitespace/format issues and it reported no problems.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69fae8a89e3883279e4d1d49686ae0d1)

title: Map supported resistor footprint shorthands in resistorProps
description: Maps supported resistor footprint shorthands like 01005, 0402, and 2512 to res01005, res0402, and res2512 in resistorProps, while preserving explicit footprints and adding tests for the new behavior.
body:
## Summary
- move resistor footprint shorthand handling into `resistorProps`
- map supported resistor footprint shorthands like `01005`, `0402`, and `2512` to `res01005`, `res0402`, and `res2512`
- use an explicit allowlist of footprinter-supported resistor sizes instead of a generic numeric pattern
- keep this behavior resistor-specific so shared footprint shorthands for other components are unchanged
- preserve explicit footprints like `kicad:R_0402_1005Metric` without rewriting them
- add tests for both shorthand mapping and passthrough behavior

title: Add krt autorouter preset
description: Adds a new autorouter preset named krt to the autorouter configuration, updating relevant types, schemas, and documentation accordingly.
body:
### Motivation
- Expose a new autorouter preset named `krt` so it can be selected in group autorouter configurations.
- Keep generated documentation and type/schema artifacts in sync with the new preset.

### Description
- Add `"krt"` to the `preset` union on `AutorouterConfig` and to the `AutorouterPreset` type in `lib/components/group.ts`.
- Add `z.enum` / `z.literal` entries for `krt` in the runtime `autorouterConfig` schema and `autorouterPreset` union.
- Update generated artifacts `generated/COMPONENT_TYPES.md` and `generated/PROPS_OVERVIEW.md` to include the new preset.
- Add a unit test in `tests/autorouter.test.ts` asserting `autorouterProp.parse("krt")` returns `"krt"`.

### Testing
- Ran the generation scripts `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`, and `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts` which completed successfully.
- Ran `bun test tests/autorouter.test.ts` and all tests passed (10 passed, 0 failed).
- Ran the typechecker with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which completed without errors.
- Ran `biome format .` / `bun run format:check` which reported no formatting fixes required.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69fc2984bafc83278ed2719cb0f3b3af)

title: Add courtyard circle support
description: Adds support for converting pcb_courtyard_circle circuit-json elements into courtyardcircle  in generated tscircuit footprints.
body:
## Summary

Adds support for converting `pcb_courtyard_circle` circuit-json elements into `<courtyardcircle />` in generated tscircuit footprints.

## What Changed

- added `pcb_courtyard_circle` handling in `generateFootprintTsx`
- emitted `<courtyardcircle pcbX={...} pcbY={...} radius={...} layer="..." />`
- extended the courtyard round-trip test to cover circle support
- bumped `circuit-json` from `^0.0.391` to `^0.0.423` so the newer courtyard circle type/accessor is available

## Why

Courtyard outlines and rects were already supported, but courtyard circles were being skipped. This made the converter incomplete for footprints that use circular courtyard geometry.

## Impact

Footprints containing `pcb_courtyard_circle` now round-trip correctly from circuit-json to generated tscircuit code and back.

## Validation

- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun test`

title: support pic_programmer schematic
description: Adds parser and serializer support for the pic_programmer.kicad_sch schematic, enabling full parsing and serialization of previously unsupported symbol and property tokens.
body:
## What changed

This PR adds parser and serializer support needed for `kicad-demos/demos/pic_programmer/pic_programmer.kicad_sch`.

The schematic uses a few newer or previously unsupported symbol and property tokens, so this change teaches the S-expression model to handle them and keeps the file round-trippable.

## Why it changed

`pic_programmer.kicad_sch` could not be fully parsed and serialized before because several tokens in its symbol and sheet property structures were missing.

The immediate blockers were:
- `in_pos_files`
- `body_style`
- `body_styles`
- property `show_name`
- property `do_not_autoplace`
- `power global` serialization

## Impact

This improves schematic compatibility for KiCad demo content and expands the parser's coverage without widening scope beyond the requested `pic_programmer.kicad_sch` path.

## Validation

- `bun test tests/sexpr/KicadSchDemos.test.ts tests/sexpr/classes/Symbol.test.ts tests/sexpr/classes/Sheet.test.ts`
- `bun run typecheck`

title: add PCB snapshot drill-hole color flag
description: Adds an opt-in pcbDrillHoleColor flag to the KiCad snapshot pipeline to ensure drill holes remain visible in KiCad 10 renders by remapping their colors before rasterizing PCB snapshots.
body:
## Summary
This PR adds an opt-in `pcbDrillHoleColor` flag to the KiCad snapshot pipeline and uses it for PCB snapshot tests so drill holes stay visible in KiCad 10 renders.



## Issue
KiCad 10's `pcb export svg` is emitting drill holes in black SVG primitives:
- circular holes as solid black filled circles
- oval/pill holes as black stroked round-cap paths

Our PCB snapshot helper composites the exported SVG onto a black background. That makes those black drill-hole shapes blend into the background, so the holes look missing in the snapshot output even though the PCB geometry is still present.

## Solution
- add `normalizePcbSvgForSnapshot(svg, pcbDrillHoleColor?)` to remap KiCad 10 drill-hole colors before rasterizing PCB snapshots
- support both circular and oval/pill drill-hole SVG representations
- thread a new opt-in `pcbDrillHoleColor?: string` flag through `takeKicadSnapshot(...)`
- update PCB snapshot call sites to pass `pcbDrillHoleColor: "white"`
- refresh the affected PCB snapshot PNGs

## Why this approach
This is a snapshot/rendering issue, not a PCB data issue. Keeping the fix in the snapshot/export pipeline means:
- we do not mutate the actual `.kicad_pcb` output
- the behavior stays explicit and opt-in for snapshot callers
- we can change the visualization policy later without touching KiCad board generation

## Validation
- `bun test tests/fixtures/take-kicad-snapshot.test.ts -t "normalizePcbSvgForSnapshot remaps both circular and oval drill holes"`
- attempted `bun test tests/pcb/basics/basics14-npth-shapes.test.tsx`, but Bun still segfaults after KiCad export in this environment

Because of that Bun crash, the updated snapshot PNGs were regenerated through the same fixture pipeline and committed with this change.

title: Connected source_port_ids need to be present for the source_trace
description: This pull request introduces a mechanism to ensure that connected source port IDs are properly tracked and associated with source traces in the PCB context. It modifies several files to add a mapping for source port IDs and updates the logic for creating source traces accordingly.

title: Add source_net support
description: Before  No source_net and multiple pcb_trace using the same source_trace_id (while inflating in core it drops all the other and picks one)  type: source_trace, source_trace_id: source_trace_2, connected_source_port_ids:  pcb_component_1_port_3, pcb_component_1_port_28, ......, pcb_component_55_port_1, , connected_source_net_ids: , display_name: GND ,   After   type: source_net, source_net_id: source_net_5v, name: 5V ,  linked to the parent source_net   type: source_trace, source_trace_id: source_trace_5v_1, connected_source_port_ids: U1_pin4, C1_pin1, connected_source_net_ids: source_net_5v, display_name: 5V   Each pcb_trace linked to there own source_trace (helps in inflation in core, and doesnt drop pcb_traces)

title: Fix source port name format
description: This pull request modifies the way source port names and pin numbers are generated in the CollectSourceTracesStage class. It replaces the previous method of obtaining the reference from footprint properties with a new method that formats the port name as pinX where X is the pin number. Additionally, it introduces a new method to parse the pin number from the pad number string.

title: Separate kicad traces into pcb_trace.route instead of turning each segment into a pcb_trace
description: Collects connected copper primitives into contiguous pcb_trace routes instead of creating separate traces for each segment.

title: Add repro arduino-uno
description: This pull request adds a repro for the Arduino Uno, including a comprehensive set of components and ports in the circuit JSON format.

title: Fix disconnected trace segment bug
description: Fixes a bug where a trace segment was incorrectly assigned to a larger node, resulting in a zero-length segment in the output SVG.
body: Stack created with GitHub Stacks CLI • Give Feedback 💬 Before the fix, findNodeIndexForRoute returned the first node that contained all route points. In this dataset, a short trace segment for source_trace_50 fit inside both a large parent node and a smaller sub-node, so the solver picked the larger one by accident. Later, when the solver clamped route endpoints to that wrong node’s bounds, one endpoint got collapsed onto the other, producing a zero-length same- layer segment in the output SVG. That logic is in lib/ HighDensityForceImproveSolver.ts:259. The fix changes that selection rule: instead of “first containing node wins,” it now scans all containing nodes and picks the smallest-area one. That means a route segment gets assigned to the tightest matching sub-node, which preserves the intended local geometry and prevents the endpoint clamp from flattening the segment. The changed logic is lib/HighDensityForceImproveSolver.ts:267. I also added a regression guard in tests/disconnected-trace- segment.snapshot.test.ts:27 that explicitly fails if any source_trace_50 route contains a degenerate same-layer segment, and then the snapshot assertion verifies the repaired geometry visually at tests/disconnected-trace- segment.snapshot.test.ts:122.

title: Introduce Interactive DRC14 Cosmos Debugger to Accelerate Solver Regression Analysis and Repair Quality
description: Adds an interactive DRC14 Cosmos Debugger for enhanced solver regression analysis and repair quality.

title: Add initial workflow configurations and project setup files
description: Adds the initial public API for rendering circuit-json boards to 3D assets and PNG snapshots, with coverage for camera control and model URL normalization.
body:
Adds the initial public API for rendering `circuit-json` boards to 3D assets and PNG snapshots, with coverage for camera control and model URL normalization.

  ## What changed

  - add `convertCircuitJsonTo3dGltf`
  - add `convertCircuitJsonTo3dGlb`
  - add `renderCircuitJsonTo3dPng`
  - add reusable camera preset support for common board viewing angles
  - normalize relative and absolute model asset URLs before conversion
  - expose a standard package entry point for downstream consumers
  - add visual regression tests for PNG output and camera preset rendering
  - add a GLB validity test to verify generated binary output

  ## Tests

  - verifies `renderCircuitJsonTo3dPng` returns a valid PNG buffer
  - verifies custom camera settings override presets
  - verifies each camera preset produces a stable rendered snapshot
  - verifies `convertCircuitJsonTo3dGlb` returns a valid GLB buffer

title: Fix false DRC missing-connection errors for multi-pad PCB ports
description: Fixes false DRC missing-connection errors for multi-pad PCB ports by allowing multiple pads to be associated with a single port and improving point-in-pad checks.
body: REPRO: [TSX CODE](https://tscircuit.com/editor?&snippet_type=board#data:application/gzip;base64,) Fixes a false positive in `checkTracesAreContiguous` where valid traces could be reported as missing a connection when a single logical PCB port is represented by multiple copper pads. This happens in custom footprints that use a small rectangular SMT pad as a routing anchor and a larger polygon SMT pad for the actual copper shape. The trace is physically connected, but the DRC checker could still report: `Trace [...] is missing a connection to smtpad[...]` **Root cause** `checkTracesAreContiguous` previously stored only one pad or plated hole per `pcb_port_id`: `Map` When multiple pads shared the same port, the later pad overwrote the earlier one. The checker then tested trace endpoints against only that single pad. Polygon SMT pads were also not handled by `isPointInPad`, so endpoints landing inside polygon copper could be treated as disconnected. **Changes** - Store all SMT pads and plated holes for each `pcb_port_id` - Treat a trace endpoint as connected if it lands in any pad or hole for the expected port - Add point-in-polygon support for `pcb_smtpad.shape === "polygon"` - Add a regression test covering a shared-port rectangular anchor plus polygon pad footprint **Why** This fixes a subtle DRC correctness issue for custom footprints built from multiple copper shapes. Users can now model composite SMT pads without getting misleading missing-connection errors when the trace is actually connected to the intended copper.

title: Clip out-of-bounds obstacles to board limits
description: Clips obstacles that cross the board edge to ensure only the part inside the board is treated as real, while ignoring those fully outside the board.
body: Stack created with GitHub Stacks CLI • Give Feedback 💬 This PR tightens how out-of-bounds obstacles are handled. Obstacles that lie fully outside the board are ignored, and obstacles that cross the board edge are clipped so only the part inside the board is treated as real. This keeps the solver output and final obstacle shapes aligned with the actual board area, while still allowing the initial view to show the original input shape for debugging.

title: Stop gap-fill expansion at board edges
description: Fixes out-of-bounds issue by ensuring the solver stops expanding at the board boundary and adds regression tests to maintain this behavior.
body: Stack created with GitHub Stacks CLI • Give Feedback 💬 This PR applies the actual fix for the main out-of-bounds issue. When the solver tries to grow open space from an edge, it now stops at the board boundary instead of extending beyond it. It also adds regression coverage so the broken cases stay fixed. In short, the previous PR prepared the boundary logic, and this one uses that groundwork to make the expansion behavior obey the board limits.

title: Prepare gap-fill logic for board boundary handling
description: Prepares the out-of-bounds fix by tightening how the solver understands board edges, ensuring shapes do not treat space outside the board as usable routing area.
body: Stack created with GitHub Stacks CLI • Give Feedback 💬 This PR prepares the out-of-bounds fix by tightening how the solver understands board edges. It teaches the system to recognize when a shape reaches the board boundary and to avoid treating space outside the board as usable routing area. This does not yet add the full expansion clamp; it lays the groundwork so the next PR can apply the actual fix cleanly. The goal here is to make boundary behavior more consistent and prevent later steps from building on the wrong assumptions.

title: Add Abse as CODEOWNER
